steps.title: Apply slope-intercept form
steps.content: A linear function can be written as $$f(x) = mx + b$$ where $m$ is the slope and $b$ is the $y$-intercept. The problem gives slope $m = 2$ and $y$-intercept $(0, -5)$, so $b = -5$.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A: $f(x) = \frac{1}{2}x - 5$ has slope $\frac{1}{2}$, not $2$. Eliminate.
  • B: $f(x) = -\frac{1}{2}x - 5$ has slope $-\frac{1}{2}$, not $2$. Eliminate.
  • C: $f(x) = -2x - 5$ has slope $-2$, not $2$. Eliminate.
  • D: $f(x) = 2x - 5$ has slope $2$ and $y$-intercept $-5$. Keep.
That matches choice D — D is correct.
